defects.symptom: Running the documented version-skew remedy (`dummyindex install --scope user`, banner in 5 shipped skills) or the /dummyindex-update procedure on a repo with a curated .context/ silently re-clusters the feature taxonomy: curated INDEX.json entries are replaced by community-N stubs and curated feature folders are orphaned from navigation/query — functionally lost. Reproduced live in a temp repo: curated `auth-flow` (INFERRED) entry replaced by `community-0` after `python3 -m dummyindex install --scope project --dir <repo>`.
defects.root_cause: installer/install.py `_auto_init_project` calls `build_all(project_root, ...)` UNCONDITIONALLY whenever `not skill_only and target_is_repo` — it has no curated-index guard, unlike `rebuild --changed` which gates on `_is_enriched_index` (context/build/incremental.py:137,167-216) and routes to `refresh_deterministic_artifacts`. enriched_refresh.py's own docstring states a full build_all on a curated index is 'silent data loss'. Meanwhile every skill banner ('If they diverge, re-run `dummyindex install --scope user`') and update/SKILL.md ('build_all(bootstrap=True), which is non-destructive' — false) route users straight into this path, with no mention of `--skill-only` or scope detection.
defects.proposed_fix: (1) Promote `_is_enriched_index` to a public `is_enriched_index` (keep in dummyindex/context/build/incremental.py; re-export). (2) In `_auto_init_project`, before `build_all`: if `is_enriched_index(project_root / '.context')`, do NOT build_all — instead call `refresh_deterministic_artifacts(project_root)`, still write the managed CLAUDE.md block (bootstrap_claude_md) and install hooks, and print 'curated .context/ detected — preserved; refreshed N deterministic artefact(s); for content updates run the reconcile procedure (/dummyindex --recouncil, council/65-reconcile.md)'. Return True. (3) Rewrite the banner line in all five SKILL.md sources to: 'If they diverge, re-run `dummyindex install --skill-only` at the scope this skill is installed at (`--scope project --dir <repo>` if this file lives under <repo>/.claude/skills/), or run /dummyindex-update.' (4) update/SKILL.md: delete the false 'non-destructive build_all' claims at lines 23 and 76 and describe the new guard (install preserves a curated index; deterministic refresh + skills + hooks only). Design decision: whether the guarded install path should also print the reconcile drift summary like rebuild --changed does (recommended; reuse the _print_enriched_summary pattern).
defects.tests_to_add: Installer test: temp git repo with curated features/INDEX.json (feature_id 'auth-flow', confidence INFERRED) + curated spec.md; run install(scope='project', project_dir=tmp); assert INDEX.json still lists 'auth-flow', no 'community-*' entry, spec.md unchanged, CLAUDE.md block + hooks still written. Second test: deterministic-only index (all community-*/EXTRACTED) still full-builds. Third: render test asserting no shipped SKILL.md contains the bare 'dummyindex install --scope user' remedy string.

defects.symptom: Every generated doc tells sessions the wrong update path: HOW_TO_USE.md says the index is 'refreshed by `dummyindex context rebuild --changed`' and 'When the index is wrong ... re-run rebuild --changed (rebuilds only the changed bits)'; all five playbooks end with 'Re-index: rebuild --changed'; the managed CLAUDE.md block claims `dummyindex context reconcile` 'folds new/changed code into the curated taxonomy' (it is a read-only report) and ends 'flag it and run rebuild --changed'. HOW_TO_USE also says 'Don't write to .context/ by hand. All files are regenerated on rebuild' — contradicting the curated in-session-update model the drift hook depends on.
defects.root_cause: Template strings predate the curated/commit-anchored model. At HEAD, `rebuild --changed` on a curated index only refreshes deterministic artefacts (verified live: 'enriched index preserved; refreshed 7 deterministic artefact(s)') and never updates feature docs; `context reconcile` writes nothing (cli/help.py:144-151 'Writes nothing'). The templates were never updated, so generated HOW_TO_USE.md, playbooks, and the managed CLAUDE.md block (also propagated into the user's global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) prescribe a command that cannot do what they claim, and forbid the in-session doc edits the drift system requires.
defects.proposed_fix: Rewrite the affected template strings: (a) instructions.py:27 → 'Deterministic backbone refreshed by `dummyindex context rebuild --changed` (preserves curated feature docs); content updates flow through the reconcile procedure (/dummyindex --recouncil — a Claude skill invocation; council/65-reconcile.md).' (b) 'When the index is wrong' → distinguish: stale deterministic artefacts → rebuild --changed; stale/missing feature docs → edit the doc in-session or run the reconcile procedure; state explicitly that rebuild --changed does NOT touch feature docs. (c) Replace the 'Don't write to .context/ by hand / All files are regenerated' bullet with the real contract: map/, tree.json, naming, source-docs are regenerated (don't hand-edit); features/<id>/*.md and conventions/*.md (except naming) are council-owned and ARE edited in-session to clear drift. (d) Playbook closing steps → 'rebuild --changed refreshes the deterministic map; if you added new files, also run the reconcile procedure so a feature owns them.' (e) bootstrap.py block → correct reconcile description ('`dummyindex context reconcile` reports the delta; the /dummyindex skill's reconcile procedure folds it in'), drop the trailing 'run rebuild --changed' remedy in favor of 'update the doc in-session / run the reconcile procedure', and add: 'When the user's explicit instruction contradicts a .context spec/plan, the user wins — note the divergence and proceed' (covers the spec-over-user evidence).
defects.tests_to_add: Update golden/text assertions for generate_how_to_use_md, generate_playbook_md, generate_managed_block: rebuild --changed no longer presented as the feature-doc refresh; reconcile procedure referenced; read-only nature of `context reconcile` stated; user-overrides-index sentence present; old 'All files are regenerated on rebuild' bullet gone.

defects.symptom: /dummyindex skill resolves ALL invocation tokens as a scope path — '/dummyindex usage chat' nearly triggered a full re-ingest; the rule says verbs are 'filler' and multi-token args 'join with /' into a path. Nothing recognizes real CLI verbs or sibling skills before scope resolution.
defects.root_cause: The 'Resolve scope + root' rule (skill.md:14-19) interprets tokens ONLY as paths: '`/dummyindex index <path>` / `/dummyindex scan <path>` / similar verb forms → still resolve `<path>` as the scope; the verb is filler' and 'Multiple non-flag tokens → join with `/` if they look like a path'. `usage` is a genuine top-level CLI verb (`dummyindex usage chat` = the /tokens report), so '/dummyindex usage chat' is mis-resolved toward an ingest instead of running the usage command.
defects.proposed_fix: Insert a rule 0 before path resolution in skills/skill.md step 1: 'If the FIRST token is a dummyindex CLI verb (`usage`, `context`, `install`, `uninstall`, `ingest`) — run that CLI command verbatim and stop; this is not a scope. If it names a sibling skill (plan/build/equip/audit/remember/update), redirect to that skill. Only treat a token as scope when it is path-like AND exists on disk; if a token is both an existing path and a CLI verb, ask one clarifying question. Never start an ingest because token interpretation failed — ambiguity stops, it does not ingest.' Narrow line 17's verb-is-filler clause to the explicit synonyms `index`/`scan` only.
defects.tests_to_add: Skill-render assertion that the rendered SKILL.md contains the verb-recognition rule and that 'the verb is filler' is scoped to index/scan only (markdown-only change; enforced by prose).

defects.symptom: Every /dummyindex-plan run in a normal environment burns two failed Task dispatches: the critique-panel table hardcodes subagent_type values `Software Architect` and `Code Reviewer` (agency-agents names) that usually don't exist; the general-purpose fallback is a parenthetical aside, so the failure path is the default path.
defects.root_cause: plan/SKILL.md step 7 ships a fixed persona table with exact `subagent_type` strings and only a parenthetical '(fall back to general-purpose if that agent type isn't available)'. Unlike the main skill — which has an explicit 'Honor agent availability' procedure (skills/skill.md:277-286: dispatch the first available of [subagent_type, *fallbacks]) — the plan skill has no resolution step, so models dispatch the named types blind and two of three Task calls error.
defects.proposed_fix: Mirror the main skill's availability rule as an explicit numbered sub-step in step 7, before the table: 'Resolve agent availability first: check the agent registry visible to you (and preflight output if present); for each critic dispatch the first available of [the named type, general-purpose] — the inlined mandate steers a general-purpose agent equally well. Do not dispatch a type you have not confirmed exists.' Reframe the table column as 'preferred `subagent_type` (only if available; otherwise general-purpose)'.
defects.tests_to_add: Skill-render assertion that the availability-resolution step text exists in the rendered plan SKILL.md.

defects.symptom: Build agents hand-wrote outputs that a `— via <tool>`-tagged checklist item was supposed to produce (the no-source canvas-to-code disaster; reverted in the consumer repo). The user hand-patched the installed copies (~/.claude/skills/dummyindex-build/SKILL.md, mtime ~2.5h after the install batch, and BOS-Mono/frontend/.claude/skills/dummyindex-build/SKILL.md) to make via-tags binding — those edits live only outside the package and the next `dummyindex install` will clobber them (installer overwrites SKILL.md unconditionally).
defects.root_cause: Shipped build/SKILL.md step 3 says a via-tag is 'a directive, not a hint' but defines no failure semantics — nothing says what happens when the tagged tool is unavailable or errors, so agents fall back to hand-implementing the output; the verify step (4) never asks for evidence the tagged tool ran. Diff confirms the hand-edits exist only in the two installed copies: 'BINDING routing, not a hint', 'Substitution is a build failure ... leave the item unticked, STOP ... never let an agent hand-write the output', a verify bullet 'confirm the tool actually ran and produced the result', a discipline bullet, plus a canvas-to-code-specific provenance gate (.canvas-to-code/state/<feature>/status.json non-null source iter).
defects.proposed_fix: Fold the GENERIC parts upstream into dummyindex/skills/build/SKILL.md: (a) retitle step 3's tag paragraph 'Honor `— via <tool>` tags — BINDING routing, not a hint' and adopt the hand-edit's bullet structure; (b) add verbatim: 'Substitution is a build failure. If the tagged tool is unavailable, errors out, or can't complete, leave the item unticked, STOP, and report — never let an agent hand-write the output the tool was supposed to produce. A hand-implemented `— via`-tagged item is a failed item even if its code works.'; (c) add to step 4: 'for a `— via <tool>`-tagged item, confirm the tool actually ran and produced the result — if the tool records run state or artifacts, read that evidence; output that bypassed the tagged tool fails verification regardless of code quality.'; (d) add the discipline bullet '`— via` tags are binding; substitution is a failed item, never a fallback.' EXCLUDE the canvas-to-code gate verbatim (it embeds another project's plugin paths) — it is generalized by clause (c). Design decisions: (1) where the project-specific gate should live for the consumer repo (a project-scope patch or equip usage-doc, not the shipped skill); (2) follow-up: installer hash-baselining of installed skill copies to warn instead of silently overwriting hand-edits (evidence shows no drift detection between scopes), mirroring equip's pristine/USER_MODIFIED model.
defects.tests_to_add: Skill-render assertions: rendered build SKILL.md contains 'Substitution is a build failure' and the verify-evidence bullet; does NOT contain 'canvas-to-code' or '.canvas-to-code/state'.

defects.symptom: CLI and hook outputs prescribe `/dummyindex --recouncil` as if it were a runnable CLI verb — agents grep `dummyindex --help`/`context --help` for a recouncil verb, find nothing, and burn multiple calls before concluding it's a skill invocation (recurred across Stop-gate firings). The gate text also orders enrich-then-place, the reverse of council/65-reconcile.md (recover → place → enrich → stamp).
defects.root_cause: The remediation strings name `/dummyindex --recouncil` with no indication it is a Claude slash-skill, not a dummyindex CLI subcommand (no recouncil verb exists in ContextSubcommand / cli/__init__.py dispatch). reconcile_gate.render_block additionally instructs 'for each drifted feature ... re-run its council enrichment; place any new files; then reconcile-stamp' — inverting 65-reconcile.md's documented order where placement of unassigned files precedes enrichment.
defects.proposed_fix: Rephrase the five strings to disambiguate skill vs CLI and match procedure order. E.g. reconcile.py:160 → 'Run the council reconcile procedure — invoke the /dummyindex Claude Code skill with --recouncil (a skill invocation, not a dummyindex CLI verb); procedure: council/65-reconcile.md.' In render_block/render_multi_block reorder: 'first place any new/unassigned files (scaffold-feature / assign-files), then re-run council enrichment for each drifted feature (`/dummyindex --recouncil <feature>` — the /dummyindex skill, not a CLI verb), then `dummyindex context reconcile-stamp`, then commit .context/ as its own dedicated commit.' Keep messages compact (they ride in hook payloads).
defects.tests_to_add: Unit tests on render_block/render_multi_block and reconcile._print_report output asserting the 'not a ... CLI verb' wording is present and 'place any new' precedes 're-run its council enrichment' in the gate reason string.

defects.symptom: Stale CLI help misleads sessions reverse-engineering the surface: `context --help` documents equipment.json as '(v2)' while equip writes schema_version 3; says `build --status` 'prints `dummyindex context rebuild --changed`' when the code prints `dummyindex context reconcile`; and omits the `equip discover` / `equip install` verbs even though the plan skill instructs using them.
defects.root_cause: Hardcoded help strings were not updated when (a) the equip manifest bumped to SCHEMA_VERSION = 3 (plugin-manager fields), (b) the build-loop closer changed to RECONCILE_HINT = 'dummyindex context reconcile' (waves.py:34; dispatch.py:160), and (c) the discover/install verbs landed (EquipVerb.DISCOVER/INSTALL wired at cli/equip/dispatch.py:96-99; flags in cli/equip/discover.py) without help entries. cli/equip/dispatch.py's module docstring also still says 'manifest v2' and its verb list omits discover/install.
defects.proposed_fix: (1) help.py:255 '(v2)' → '(v3)' — preferably interpolate the constant (import SCHEMA_VERSION from dummyindex.context.domains.equip and format USAGE) so it can never drift again. (2) help.py:307 → 'prints `dummyindex context reconcile` (close the loop via the reconcile procedure, council/65-reconcile.md)'. (3) Add help entries for `equip discover "<query>" [--json]` and `equip install <plugin>@<marketplace> [--scope project|local|user] [--usage-doc PATH|--skip-usage-doc] [--yes]` (flags confirmed in cli/equip/discover.py:176-359). (4) Fix the dispatch.py docstring: v2 → v3, add discover/install to the verb surface list.
defects.tests_to_add: Help-consistency unit test: assert f'(v{SCHEMA_VERSION})' appears in cli.help.USAGE; assert waves.RECONCILE_HINT appears in USAGE and 'rebuild --changed' is absent from the build --status description; assert 'equip discover' and 'equip install' appear in USAGE.

defects.symptom: Consumers of the trivial-filter doc read feature.json expecting `member_count`/`entry_point_count`/`file_count`/`flow_count` and crash on None (TypeError in the evidence session); flow_count appears permanently unpopulated because the field never existed in feature.json. Separately, a consumer guessed `id` in features/INDEX.json and hit KeyError — entries use `feature_id` under a top-level `features` key, which HOW_TO_USE.md never states.
defects.root_cause: 18-filter-trivial.md keys the filter on `member_count < 3` / `entry_point_count == 0` / `file_count == 1` without saying WHERE those fields live. Feature.to_dict (models.py:69-81) writes only the raw lists (members/files/entry_points/flow_ids) to feature.json; the count fields exist solely in features/INDEX.json entries (builder.py:285-298). HOW_TO_USE.md's navigation table points at INDEX.json/feature.json but documents no field names; only features/HOW_TO_NAVIGATE.md mentions feature_id, and it never names the `features` wrapper key.
defects.proposed_fix: (1) Amend 18-filter-trivial.md 'When the filter fires': 'The count fields live in the feature's entry in `features/INDEX.json` (NOT in feature.json — that file carries the raw `members`/`files`/`entry_points`/`flow_ids` lists; compute len() if you read it directly).' (2) One-line schema note in the HOW_TO_USE template's feature row (instructions.py:37): 'INDEX.json entries live under a top-level `features` key and are keyed `feature_id` (not `id`), with name/summary plus member_count/file_count/entry_point_count/flow_count.' Design note: do NOT add count fields to feature.json — that duplicates state every placement op would have to maintain; the doc fix is the right surface.
defects.tests_to_add: Text assertions on the updated 18-filter-trivial.md and generate_how_to_use_md output ('feature_id', `features` key documented); a schema regression test asserting Feature.to_dict emits no *_count keys (locks the documented contract).

defects.symptom: /dummyindex-update cannot pin a version — it always resolves the latest GitHub tag (or main), so a session that must update to a user-chosen version (e.g. exactly 0.24.0) has to bypass the skill and hand-roll all layers.
defects.root_cause: update/SKILL.md step 1 only documents resolving the latest release tag with a main fallback; no invocation argument is defined for a target version, and the idempotency check compares only against latest.
defects.proposed_fix: Add an optional positional argument: '/dummyindex-update [<version|tag>]'. In step 1: 'If the user passed a version/tag, use it as <REF> verbatim (accept with or without the v prefix), skip latest-resolution, and compare the installed version against IT for the idempotency check.' Update frontmatter description/triggers to mention pinning ('update dummyindex to 0.24.0'). Keep --force semantics unchanged.
defects.tests_to_add: Skill-render assertion that the rendered update SKILL.md documents the optional version argument and the pinned-REF rule.

defects.symptom: The plan step emits human-decision items (settle the RLS/tenant-isolation model, resolve DECISIONS.md open questions) and main-session-MCP-grounded items into checklist.md, and the build skill's literal loop dispatches whatever the CLI emits via the Task tool 'as-is' — the user interrupted the moment a decision gate was offered to a subagent; that build never progressed past 0/27.
defects.root_cause: Neither skill distinguishes 'decision/approval items needing user input or main-session-only MCP tools' from 'implementable code items'. The plan skill's wave-derivation step has no rule keeping unresolved decisions out of the checklist; the build skill's step 3 mandates dispatch-as-emitted, and step 4's blocked-path ('a decision the user must make') only triggers AFTER a dispatch attempt.
defects.proposed_fix: Plan skill step 8: add — 'Open decisions never become checklist items. Resolve them in spec.md (an `## Open questions` block + a user question) before deriving the checklist; if genuinely deferred, tag the item `— gate: user` so build escalates instead of dispatching.' Build skill step 3: add — 'Before dispatching, scan the wave for items that are decisions/approvals or require tools only the main session has (e.g. hosted MCP servers): handle those IN the main session (ask the user / run the calls yourself), never dispatch them to a subagent; treat `— gate: user` as the explicit marker.' Design decision: tag syntax (`— gate: user` proposed); the build CLI passes item text through opaquely today, so no CLI change is required.
defects.tests_to_add: Skill-render text assertions for the new rules in both rendered SKILL.md files; optionally a buildloop test confirming an item with a '— gate: user' suffix round-trips unmangled through parse_checklist/--check.

not_confirmed: `rebuild --changed` re-shatters the curated taxonomy at HEAD — NOT reproduced: the non-destructive guard (context/build/incremental.py:137 `_is_enriched_index` → refresh_deterministic_artifacts) preserved a curated INFERRED index in a live temp-repo test ('enriched index preserved; refreshed 7 deterministic artefact(s)'). The destruction I COULD reproduce is `dummyindex install` (defect 1) and the explicitly-warned `rebuild --full`/bare `rebuild`. The C1 cluster owns any residual rebuild-path bug; C10's confirmed share is the doc/templating defect (defect 2).

Build loop ticking boxes from subagent self-reports (0.15.0 session) — fixed at HEAD: build/SKILL.md step 4 now specifies a concrete independent verification procedure (run tests once per wave, read claimed diffs, attribute failures, one verdict per item).

Generated checklist with `## Phase N` headings causing undefined build behavior — defined and safe at HEAD: parse_checklist (context/domains/buildloop/checklist.py:62-79) treats non-Wave headings as closing the wave, so items degrade to serial singleton groups; plan/SKILL.md:74 documents that only Wave/Group headings open parallel groups.

reconcile-gate command undiscoverable from skill docs — largely addressed at HEAD: council/65-reconcile.md:11 names `dummyindex context reconcile-gate` and cli/help.py:48-57 documents it; the evidence session ran an older installed skill copy (version-skew family, mitigated by defect 1's safe resync).

Advisor citing an 'AskUserQuestion sanction' the installed v0.15.0 plan skill lacked — the current plan skill also contains no such sanction (grep empty); the advisor asserted a provision that never existed. Version-skew/hallucination, no repo text to fix beyond defect 1's resync path.

Installed dummyindex-equip skill missing the skills.sh / two-discovery-channels section — install-state staleness only: repo HEAD's dummyindex/skills/equip/SKILL.md contains 9 'skills.sh' references (commit 755e59b); nothing to fix in the repo beyond the resync story in defect 1.

User's explicit source (obra/superpowers) silently substituted during equip discover — the failure is in equip discover's source resolution, which belongs to the equip cluster, not C10; only noted here.
Documented .context navigation path skipped (agent used find/grep, never read HOW_TO_USE.md) — behavioral/enforcement gap with no code-level root cause in this cluster's modules; no fix proposed.

Plan-only sessions lacking a rule for when canonical docs (BACKLOG/DECISIONS/feature specs) may be edited pre-approval — genuine doc gap but no confirmed code defect; partially covered by defect 2's template rewrite (the curated-layer editing contract).
quick_wins: cli/help.py:255 — change 'equipment.json (v2)' to '(v3)' (or interpolate SCHEMA_VERSION from dummyindex.context.domains.equip).
cli/help.py:307 — change build --status help from 'prints `dummyindex context rebuild --changed`' to 'prints `dummyindex context reconcile`' to match waves.py:34 RECONCILE_HINT.
cli/equip/dispatch.py:1-17 — docstring: 'manifest v2' → v3; add `discover` and `install` to the verb-surface list (they are wired at lines 96-99).

dummyindex/skills/plan/SKILL.md steps 4-5 — add one sentence: 'Read each CLI-scaffolded file (spec.md/plan.md/checklist.md) before overwriting it — the Write tool refuses to write a file the session has not Read.' (evidence: first Write to scaffolded plan.md rejected).

dummyindex/skills/skill.md Phase 1 'What you get' list — mention the Stop reconcile-gate hook alongside the SessionStart drift hook, naming `dummyindex context reconcile-gate`, so sessions stop hand-rolling duplicate Stop-hook gates.
dummyindex/context/output/instructions.py:37 — add INDEX.json field names to the HOW_TO_USE feature row: entries under a top-level `features` key, keyed `feature_id` (not `id`).
